steps.title: Set the sides equal and simplify
steps.content:
$6ax - 14a = 6ax - 18a - 24$
Subtract $6ax$ from both sides: $-14a = -18a - 24$
Add $18a$ to both sides: $4a = -24$
Divide by 4: $a = -6$

steps.title: Verify: does a = -6 give infinitely many solutions?
steps.content:
Substitute $a = -6$:
Left: $6(-6)x - 14(-6) = -36x + 84$
Right: $6(-6)x - 18(-6) - 24 = -36x + 108 - 24 = -36x + 84$
Left = Right for all $x$, confirming infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 0 only: Substituting $a = 0$ gives $0 = -24$, a contradiction. ✗
  • Choice B — -6 only: Derived algebraically above; $a = -6$ is the unique value making the equation an identity. ✓
  • Choice C — Any real number: Any value other than $a=-6$ leaves a false constant equation after the $x$ terms cancel, so the equation has no solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — No real number: We found $a = -6$ works, so this is false. ✗
B is correct.
